caption:
June botanical rotation of Driftless Gin is in the tank. This run leans on coriander we toasted in-house, fresh lemon balm, and a little Wisconsin juniper from a grower near Spring Green. Bottling Friday, on the shelf Saturday.

#driftlessgin #craftgin #madisonwi

Saturday booth at the Dane County Farmers' Market. Bottles, Glencairn sets, and honest answers about how bourbon gets made. Swipe for the 6 a.m. setup nobody photographs on purpose.

Capitol Square, 8 to 1. #dcfm #madisonwi #craftspirits

Steamed rice, koji, patience. The rice spirit project keeps moving — Midwest-grown rice from the Driftless, koji from @madisonkojiworks, and a lot of careful temperature logs. No release date yet. We'll tell you when it's right.

#koji #ricespirit #fermentation

Quick walk through the rickhouse. The 2022 fills crossed the four-year mark this spring, which means the next flagship bottling is coming out of these rows. Angels took their share. We kept the rest.

#bourbon #rickhouse #barrelaged

Bottling day for Heartland Rye batch 31. Same mash bill since 2017: 70% rye from Sauk County, the rest malted barley. Tyler ran the proofing, Cody and Renata ran the line, and the label gun only jammed twice. A good day.

#rye #bottlingday #smallbatch

Patio season is officially open at 1402 Williamson. Tasting flights, gin and tonics for the table, and a zero-proof tonic-and-lime that gets poured with the same care as everything else. Thu–Sun.

#patioseason #tastingroom #willystreet

Tyler checking gravity on this week's bourbon wash. Started at 1.062, fermenting clean at 84°F. The hydrometer doesn't care about your feelings, which is exactly why we trust it.

#fermentation #mashbill #distillerylife

Gift sets back in stock before the weekend — bourbon, two Glencairns, and barrel-stave coasters Alana refuses to let us discontinue because she's right. Tasting room or online.

#giftset #madisonmade #shopsmall

May's Driftless Gin botanicals, laid out before they hit the basket: juniper, coriander, orris, fresh rhubarb from a farm outside Mount Horeb. The rhubarb is subtle. That's the point.

#driftlessgin #botanicals #seasonal

Heads, hearts, tails — 90 seconds on how we make cuts on the spirit run, and why the middle of the run is the only part that goes in a barrel. Sound on if you want to hear the parrot running.

#distilling #cuts #spiritrun

New oak day. Twelve char #3 barrels in from Midwest Barrel Co., rolled down the ramp the old way. Swipe to see the inside of a fresh char — it looks like a campfire and smells like the next four years of our lives.

#cooperage #newoak #bourbon

Wholesale spotlight: @osteriagrazia has poured our bourbon in their house old fashioned since 2019. Marco's team treats it with more respect than we probably deserve. Go eat the tajarin.

#madisonrestaurants #wholesale #oldfashioned

Save the date: October 17, 2026. Heartland Spirits turns ten. We started in 2016 with one still, a borrowed forklift, and a spreadsheet of doubts. Party details this summer — tasting room, live music, the whole block invited.

#10years #anniversary #madisonwi

Corn delivery from Kelby Farms in Sauk County. Our bourbon mash bill is 70% of exactly this. Grain prices keep climbing, so it matters more than ever that we know the family growing it.

#graintoglass #wisconsincorn #mashbill

Rice spirit R&D, month nine. Short video of koji going onto steamed rice — 36 hours in the incubation room, turned by hand every 8. This is the slowest project we've ever run and the most interesting.

#koji #ricespirit #rnd

Bottle club spring allocation ships this week — a tasting-room-only rye finished four months in a cherry wood barrel we've been quietly experimenting with. 180 members, 180 bottles. Math is unforgiving.

#bottleclub #limitedrelease #rye

Glencairn sets and caps restocked. The logo tee in 'barrel char' gray sold out faster than the actual whiskey last month, which tells us something about our priorities or yours.

#merch #glencairn #distillerymerch

Single barrel selection day with the crew from @starliquor_madison. Five barrels sampled by spit method, lots of arguing about barrel 47 vs barrel 52, one clear winner. Their pick hits Willy Street in April. Swipe for the thief work.

#singlebarrel #barrelpick #bourbon

Recap from Saturday's chocolate and bourbon pairing with the folks from a certain Monroe Street chocolatier. Sold out two weeks ahead. Emma and Dev ran the floor like pros. We'll do it again next winter.

#tastingevent #pairing #madisonevents

The tasting room in February: snow on Williamson Street, barrel-stave shelves, and the radiator doing its best. Quietest month of the year and honestly a nice time to visit if you like asking questions.

#tastingroom #wintermadison #willystreet

January at a distillery: half production, half paperwork. TTB Form 5110.40 doesn't age in oak but it does take longer every year. Behind every bottle is a spreadsheet nobody toasts to.

#distillerylife #ttb #behindthescenes

First spirit run of 2026: Prairie Corn Vodka. Triple distilled, charcoal polished, still $26 because vodka shouldn't require a loan. The still room was 54°F this morning and we'd still rather be here.

#vodka #craftvodka #newyear

Holiday tasting recap: 240 guests over two nights, gift sets gone by 7 p.m., and Alana's mulled cider (zero proof, all spice) outsold expectations two to one. Thanks, Madison. See you in the new year. Swipe for the room before doors.

#holidaytasting #madisonevents #shopsmall

Cask Strength Bourbon 2025 Limited is out. 118.6 proof, barrels 12 and 19 from the 2021 fills, no chill filtration, nothing added. 600 bottles. When it's gone it's gone, and it's going.

#caskstrength #limitedrelease #bourbon
